description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages named test and dockerfile and set the variable DOCKER_DRIVER to overlay. In the test stage add a test-entrypoint job that uses the ubuntu:artful Docker image, installs shellcheck with apt-get -qq update && apt-get -qq -y install shellcheck, and then runs shellcheck --exclude=SC2086 entrypoint.sh. In the dockerfile stage add a build_docker job that uses the docker:git image with a docker:dind service, logs in to the registry via docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY, builds the image with docker build --pull -t ${CI_PROJECT_NAME}:${CI_BUILD_TAG:-latest} ., tags it with docker tag ${CI_PROJECT_NAME}:${CI_BUILD_TAG:-latest} ${CI_REGISTRY}/${CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE}/${CI_PROJECT_NAME}:${CI_BUILD_TAG:-latest}, and pushes it with docker push ${CI_REGISTRY}/${CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE}/${CI_PROJECT_NAME}:${CI_BUILD_TAG:-latest}. The pipeline does not define any caches, artifacts, or conditional rules.
